page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ine.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor is an Intel Xeon Sapphire Rapids model. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core.

This instance does not include any local storage, requiring the attachment of external block storage for persistent data. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 44 Gbps. The 1:2 ratio of vCPUs to memory classifies this instance as a compute-optimized resource, prioritizing processing density over memory capacity.

The dedicated CPU allocation and high network throughput make this instance type appropriate for compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage and the specialized compute-to-memory ratio allow organizations to scale processing power independently of storage capacity, optimizing resource allocation for CPU-bound tasks.

It is equipped with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 44 Gbps but does not include local storage, requiring external block storage solutions. With its dedicated CPU allocation and high network throughput, this instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio allows for cost-efficient scaling of processing power without paying for excess memory or unneeded local storage.
